pi_items.source: setup_qgis.sh lines 104-115 creates this file with two LineString features
Specified in task.json and setup_task.sh line 12-13 ensures the directory exists

Calculated from geometry: Road 1 (coordinates: [[-122.5, 37.5], [-122.3, 37.6], [-122.1, 37.7]]) produces 10 points; Road 2 (coordinates: [[-122.4, 37.5], [-122.4, 37.8]]) produces 7 points at 0.05 degree spacing
Calculated total of 17 points falls within the 8-30 range
Input file created by setup_qgis.sh specifies CRS as urn:ogc:def:crs:OGC:1.3:CRS84, which is WGS84; EPSG:4326 is the EPSG code for WGS84 with longitude-latitude ordering
Specified in task.json description (line 6) and metadata field; units are in layer units (degrees)
Verified in setup_qgis.sh lines 109-112; contains exactly 2 LineString features: 'Road 1' and 'Road 2'
GeoJSON CRS property in setup_qgis.sh line 108; represents WGS84 using OGC naming convention

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetic GIS data generated by setup_qgis.sh. The input file sample_lines.geojson contains 2 LineString features representing roads in the San Francisco Bay Area (coordinates: Road 1 from -122.5,37.5 to -122.1,37.7; Road 2 from -122.4,37.5 to -122.4,37.8) in WGS84 coordinate system. Using the QGIS 'Points along geometry' tool with a 0.05 degree spacing parameter, approximately 17 points are expected to be generated (10 from Road 1 and 7 from Road 2), falling within the claimed range of 8-30 points. The expected output is a GeoJSON file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/inspection_points.geojson with Point geometry features in EPSG:4326/WGS84.
